ucs.title: [LLM] Gorgon Group maldoc chain: Office app spawning hidden PowerShell to URL-shortener payload host
ucs.description: Hunts Gorgon Group's signature initial-access-to-stager chain where a weaponized Office attachment (Word/Excel) launches PowerShell with hidden window flags and reaches out to bit.ly/pastebin/blogspot to retrieve a stage-2 commodity RAT (njRAT, NanoCore, Remcos, RevengeRAT, Lokibot). This combination of Office parent + hidden PowerShell + URL-shortener egress is documented across multiple Subaat / Gorgon Group operations against UK/US/Spanish/Russian government targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| where match(process,"(?i)(-w(in)?\s*(1|hidden)|-windowstyle\s*hidden|-nop|-noni|-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String|DownloadString|DownloadFile|IEX|Invoke-Expression)") 
| join type=left host pid [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(Web.url) as url from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="powershell.exe" OR All_Traffic.process_name="powershell.exe" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id host 
    | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.process_id as pid 
  ] 
| eval suspect_host=if(match(url,"(?i)(bit\.ly|tinyurl|j\.mp|t\.co|goo\.gl|is\.gd|pastebin\.com|paste\.ee|hastebin|blogspot\.com|blogger\.com|drive\.google\.com|onedrive|raw\.githubusercontent)"),1,0) 
| where suspect_host=1 OR cmdline_match=1 
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) 
| table firstTime lastTime host Processes.user parent_process_name process_name process dest url
ucs.kql:
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let susShorteners = dynamic(["bit.ly","tinyurl.com","j.mp","t.co","goo.gl","is.gd","pastebin.com","paste.ee","hastebin.com","blogspot.com","blogger.com"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-w(in)?\s*(1|hidden)|-windowstyle\s+hidden|-nop|-noni|-enc(odedcommand)?|FromBase64String|DownloadString|DownloadFile|IEX|Invoke-Expression|Net\.WebClient)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
    | extend host=tostring(parse_url(RemoteUrl).Host)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (susShorteners) or host has_any (susShorteners) or RemoteIPType=="Public"
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, NetPid=InitiatingProcessId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
) on DeviceName
| where ProcessId == NetPid and abs(datetime_diff('second', NetTime, Timestamp)) < 120
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
ucs.rationale: Unit 42's Subaat/Gorgon reporting (Aug 2018) and follow-on tracking show Gorgon Group consistently chains macro-laden Office lures → cmd/PowerShell with hidden-window + base64 → fetch from bit.ly or blogspot to stage njRAT/NanoCore/Remcos. The triple-correlation (Office parent + obfuscated PS flags + shortener egress within 120s on the same PID) is far narrower than a generic 'Office spawns PowerS

ucs.title: [LLM] Gorgon Group .NET LOLBIN process hollowing (MSBuild/RegAsm/InstallUtil) loading commodity RAT
ucs.description: Detects Gorgon Group's hallmark stage-2 evasion: hollowing the unmanaged child of a Microsoft .NET trusted binary (RegAsm.exe, MSBuild.exe, InstallUtil.exe, AddInProcess.exe) to execute njRAT/NanoCore/RevengeRAT/Remcos in-memory while inheriting the LOLBIN's signed reputation. The combination of a script-host or Office/PowerShell parent → .NET LOLBIN child running from %APPDATA%/%TEMP% → outbound C2 is highly characteristic of Gorgon's loaders.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe","MSBuild.exe","InstallUtil.exe","AddInProcess.exe","AddInProcess32.exe","AppLaunch.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path Processes.process_id 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| eval susParent=if(match(parent_process_name,"(?i)^(powershell|cmd|wscript|cscript|mshta|winword|excel|outlook|explorer)\.exe$"),1,0) 
| eval susPath=if(match(process_path,"(?i)\\\\(AppData|Temp|ProgramData|Public|Users\\\\Public)\\\\"),1,0) 
| eval bareCmd=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)(RegAsm|RegSvcs|MSBuild|InstallUtil|AddInProcess|AppLaunch)\.exe\s*$"),1,0) 
| where susParent=1 OR susPath=1 OR bareCmd=1 
| join type=left host process_id [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("RegAsm.exe","RegSvcs.exe","MSBuild.exe","InstallUtil.exe","AddInProcess.exe","AppLaunch.exe") AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id 
    | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.process_id as process_id
  ] 
| where isnotnull(c2_ip) OR susParent=1 
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) 
| table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process_name process_path cmdline c2_ip c2_port
ucs.kql:
let dotnetLolbins = dynamic(["regasm.exe","regsvcs.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","addinprocess.exe","addinprocess32.exe","applaunch.exe"]);
let susParents = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (dotnetLolbins)
| extend susParent = iff(InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (susParents), 1, 0)
| extend susPath  = iff(FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)\\(AppData|Temp|ProgramData|Public)\\", 1, 0)
| extend bareCmd  = iff(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(regasm|regsvcs|msbuild|installutil|addinprocess|applaunch)\.exe\s*$", 1, 0)
| where susParent == 1 or susPath == 1 or bareCmd == 1
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (dotnetLolbins)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, NetPid=InitiatingProcessId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl
) on DeviceName
| where (ProcessId == NetPid and abs(datetime_diff('second', NetTime, Timestamp)) < 600) or isempty(NetPid)
| union (
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (dotnetLolbins) or RegistryValueData matches regex @"(?i)(regasm|msbuild|installutil|addinprocess|applaunch)\.exe"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData
)
ucs.rationale: Unit 42 and Morphisec analyses of Gorgon/Subaat campaigns repeatedly show their .NET-based loaders (often built with derivatives of Aspire/CyaX) using process hollowing into RegAsm.exe and MSBuild.exe to evade reputation-based AV — RegAsm running with no arguments, from %AppData%, parented by powershell.exe or wscript.exe, and beaconing to dynamic-DNS C2 is a near-pathognomonic Gorgon pattern. Pur
